project_metadata.override_scope_note: Bun 1.1+ preserves scoped selectors. The key 'hono > zod' must appear verbatim in parsed override metadata — NOT flattened to bare 'zod'. Bun 1.0.x would have stored this as 'zod' only.

warnings: Bun 1.1+ honors scoped selectors. This is the correct modern behavior. The override applies only to the hono→zod edge. If Mend's parser treats the scoped selector as a flat override (like Bun 1.0), every zod in the tree would be 3.22.0 — that would be the WRONG answer for this Bun version.

hono@4.12.18 has ZERO runtime dependencies (verified against npm registry). The scoped override 'hono > zod': '3.22.0' has no hono→zod edge to apply — zod does not appear in this lockfile. This is expected and correct. The probe validates that: (1) Mend records the scoped override declaration in project_metadata.overrides with the composite key 'hono > zod' verbatim, and (2) Mend does NOT fabricate a zod entry in the dep tree because the override exists.

Paired probe: bun-overrides-1.0-flat-probe (#11) at https://github.com/mend-detection-qa/bun-overrides-1.0-flat-probe. Same package.json; the difference is Bun version + override scope semantics. Probe #11 uses Bun 1.0.36 which degrades 'hono > zod' to flat 'zod'. Probe #12 (this probe) uses Bun 1.1.30 which preserves the scoped form. Compare project_metadata.overrides between the two probes — they should differ in key format.

Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. No .whitesource file is emitted — manual tool version pinning via scanSettings.versioning is impossible for this ecosystem. Detection is lockfile-driven (bun.lock JSONC static parsing). If Mend cannot parse bun.lock text JSONC format, the entire dep tree will be empty.
The JSONC lockfile includes inline comments (// ...) and trailing commas per the Bun 1.1 text format spec. Mend's parser MUST tolerate these or will crash on first comment line.

incorrect_behavior_if_flat_override_appl.description: If Mend treats the scoped override as flat (Bun 1.0 behavior), it might incorrectly add zod@3.22.0 to the tree even though hono does not depend on zod. This would be a false-positive dependency insertion.

incorrect_behavior_if_override_stripped.description: If Mend ignores the overrides block entirely, project_metadata.overrides will be empty. This means downstream consumers cannot detect that an override was declared, masking the scoped-vs-flat semantic difference.
